pi_items.metadata_value: One input can feed two output feeds using Reset to Original between branches
pi_items.verified_value: Confirmed: this is a documented community pattern for splitting bidirectional meter readings
pi_items.source: OpenEnergyMonitor community forum (community.openenergymonitor.org/t/setting-up-emonpi-with-mulitple-solar-inverters-how-best-to-create-feeds/2043): 'You can use reset to zero in between the import and the export calcs to create multiple feed outputs from a single input'; process_processlist.php Reset to Original restores proc_initialvalue stored at start of process list execution

pi_items.metadata_value: Either import-first or export-first ordering is valid
pi_items.verified_value: Confirmed: export_result.sh records grid_process_order as a neutral list (line 92) but does not enforce any specific ordering. Both orderings are functionally equivalent as long as Reset to Original separates the two branches.

privileged_info_summary: This task requires configuring a bidirectional grid metering pipeline in Emoncms at http://localhost (admin/admin). Two inputs exist under node 'building': grid_meter (signed power: 4500W import, -1200W export, 3800W import) and solar_pv (CT under-reads by 2x: values 1400, 2100, 1850 are half of true power). The correct solution requires: (A) On solar_pv: add Scale process (ID 2, arg 2.0) to correct CT ratio, then Log to Feed (ID 1) to 'solar_power' feed, then Power to kWh (ID 4) to 'solar_energy_kwh' feed — 3 processes total. (B) On grid_meter: build a branching pipeline using Reset to Original (ID 37) to split signed power into import and export paths. One valid ordering: Allow Positive (ID 24, no arg) to zero out negative values, Log to Feed (ID 1) to 'grid_import_power', Power to kWh (ID 4) to 'grid_import_kwh', Reset to Original (ID 37, no arg) to restore the original signed value, Allow Negative (ID 25, no arg) to zero out positive values, Scale (ID 2, arg -1) to make export values positive, Log to Feed (ID 1) to 'grid_export_power', Power to kWh (ID 4) to 'grid_export_kwh' — 8 processes total. The alternative ordering (export-first then import-first) is equally valid. The key insight is that Reset to Original (ID 37) must separate the two branches. All feeds should use PHPFina engine (ID 5) with 10-second interval. A dashboard named 'Net Zero Tracker' must be created with at least 5 widgets: solar power dial (0-20kW range), grid import power dial (0-50kW range), grid export power value display, cumulative solar energy kWh value, and cumulative grid import kWh value. Process IDs verified against official Emoncms GitHub source (emoncms/emoncms/Modules/process/process_processlist.php). The branching pipeline pattern is documented on OpenEnergyMonitor community forums as the standard approach for splitting bidirectional meter readings.
